title: High-dimensional concentration
summary: Concentration inequalities for high-dimensional random objects — sub-Gaussian maxima, the matrix Bernstein inequality for sums of independent random matrices, and the Hanson-Wright bound for quadratic forms.

theorems.name: Matrix Bernstein inequality
theorems.blurb: For a sum of independent, centered, Hermitian random matrices with spectral norm bounded by R and total variance σ², the operator norm of the sum has a Bernstein-type sub-exponential tail.

theorems.name: Rectangular matrix Bernstein inequality
theorems.blurb: The matrix Bernstein bound extended to sums of independent centered rectangular p×q random matrices via Hermitian dilation, with variance the max of the two one-sided second-moment norms.

theorems.name: Sub-Gaussian maximum tail bound
theorems.blurb: A union bound giving a sub-Gaussian tail for the ℓ∞ norm (coordinate-wise maximum) of a centered sub-Gaussian random vector.

theorems.name: Expected sub-Gaussian maximum bound
theorems.blurb: The expected coordinate-wise maximum of a centered sub-Gaussian random vector grows only like σ times the square root of the log dimension.

theorems.name: Hanson-Wright inequality
theorems.blurb: A quadratic form of a centered, independent-coordinate, sub-Gaussian random vector concentrates around its mean with a mixed sub-Gaussian / sub-exponential tail governed by the Frobenius and operator norms of A.

theorems.name: Hanson-Wright inequality (isotropic)
theorems.blurb: The isotropic specialization of Hanson-Wright, where the centering term is exactly the trace of A.
